mechanism_narrative: Declining unemployment rate signals strengthening labor market and rising consumer confidence, which drives increased discretionary spending and corporate earnings growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 6.6% in Jan 2014 showing post-crisis recovery trend
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX produces positive 20d returns in fewer than 55% of instances when UNRATE declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts signal strengthening residential construction demand and forward-looking economic confidence. This mechanism operates with a 60-day lag as construction activity translates into employment and supply-chain spending, driving equity valuations higher.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 999k units in Dec 2013, recovering from crisis lows
regime_hint: housing recovery cycle in post-financial-crisis expansion
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following HOUST acceleration > 5% are not significantly positive (mean return < 0.5% or t-stat < 1.5), or Rank IC < 0.025 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate near zero combined with stable or declining inflation signals accommodative monetary stance that reduces equity risk premium and supports valuation multiples. This always-on mechanism predicts positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS remains below 0.25% and CPIAUCSL growth is subdued.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.07% and CPIAUCSL at 234.6 in early 2014
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary policy with subdued inflation
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and CPIAUCSL YoY growth < 2%, SPX 5-day returns are not significantly positive (mean < 0.2% or t-stat < 1.2), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth signals expanding manufacturing capacity and real economic activity. When INDPRO accelerates above trend, it indicates strengthening corporate profitability and capital expenditure, driving positive equity returns over 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.0 in Jan 2014 showing post-crisis recovery
regime_hint: supply-side expansion with accelerating industrial activity
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following INDPRO acceleration > 0.3% are not significantly positive (mean < 0.3% or t-stat < 1.3), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth combined with rising payroll employment signals robust consumer demand and labor income growth. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 20-day returns when both RSAFS and PAYEMS accelerate simultaneously, indicating broad-based demand strength.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and PAYEMS both show month-over-month growth > 0.2%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137.5M and RSAFS at 427.8B in Jan 2014
regime_hint: synchronized demand expansion with employment and consumption growth
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and PAYEMS accelerate > 0.2% MoM, SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive (mean < 0.4% or t-stat < 1.4), or interaction term is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid payroll employment growth signals tight labor market and rising wage pressures, which compress corporate profit margins and increase discount rates. This mechanism predicts negative 20-day returns when PAYEMS growth exceeds 0.4% month-over-month, indicating late-cycle overheating.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 137.5M in Jan 2014 with moderate growth trajectory
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with accelerating wage pressures
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns following PAYEMS growth > 0.4% MoM are not significantly negative (mean > -0.2% or t-stat > -1.2), or Rank IC < 0.015 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation measured by CPIAUCSL rising above 2.5% year-over-year signals monetary tightening pressure and rising real discount rates. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts negative 60-day returns when inflation accelerates, as the Fed is forced to raise rates and compress equity valuations.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 234.6 in Dec 2013 with moderate inflation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rising inflation expectations
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns following CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% are not significantly negative (mean > 0% or t-stat > -1.0), or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth measured by GDPC1 combined with declining unemployment signals broad-based economic expansion. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 60-day returns when both GDP growth accelerates and unemployment falls, indicating sustainable demand-driven expansion.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth > 2% and UNRATE declines > 0.2pp over preceding quarter
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 15965.6B and UNRATE at 6.6% in Q4 2013
regime_hint: synchronized expansion with GDP acceleration and labor market tightening
failure_condition: When GDPC1 QoQ growth > 2% and UNRATE declines > 0.2pp, SPX 60-day forward returns are not significantly positive (mean < 0.5% or t-stat < 1.3), or interaction coefficient is not statistically significant at p < 0.05.
